task.title: Add deterministic area-contract discovery and goal-check reporting for changed paths.

context.problem_statement: Installed target repos need a cheap deterministic report that maps changed files to declared area contracts and exposes aligned, extension, conflict, and unknown states for AGW-090 goal-alignment task packets.
background: AGW-090 added required goal_alignment fields to source task packets.
repo-contract-kit owns installed commands, target-repo scripts, managed templates, and Make targets.
scripts/repo_contract_kit.py already has read-only CLI report patterns for status, doc-impact, agent-preflight, task-packet, and backlog operations.
scripts/agent_start.py and scripts/agent_task_ready.py already collect changed files for startup and handoff gates.

Installed prompt/schema snapshots under templates/profiles/review-prompts/ and templates/common/task-packet.schema.json may need refresh so generated task packets can carry the AGW-090 goal-alignment contract.
non_goals: Do not build a planning app, hosted service, or semantic code ownership database.
Do not make AGENTS.md the place for detailed area rules; use a structured installed config/template.
Do not require target repos to have perfect area contracts on first run; report unknown explicitly.
Do not change source prompt wording in agent-workflow-kit beyond backlog closeout evidence for this implemented kit item.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit responsible for installed target-repo execution surfaces that make agent-workflow-kit contracts usable without broad manual rereads.

area_contracts.purpose: Installed CLI and target-repo scripts that emit deterministic harness reports.
Managed target-repo templates for Make targets, schemas, docs, and local agent workflow config.
Regression coverage for installed CLI, installer, startup, and readiness behavior.
Source-side backlog and packet records for cross-repo workflow stack work.

stop_conditions: Stop if implementation requires changing canonical prompt source in agent-workflow-kit rather than vendored install snapshots.
Stop if the proposed report needs external services, hosted CI, or semantic LLM classification to work.
Stop if generated task-packet scaffolds cannot remain valid when area contracts are missing; missing contracts must report unknown.

protected_files: hosted GitHub Actions behavior unless only docs mention the local report
expected_outputs: Structured installed area-contract config/template with repo goal and path contracts.
CLI report that maps changed files to area contracts and emits text plus JSON.
Installed Make target for the report.
Report states distinguish aligned, extends, conflict, and unknown.
agent-start includes or references the current goal-check report for changed files.
Task-packet scaffolds include goal-alignment defaults from the report or explicit unknowns.
agent-task-ready reports goal-check status for actual changed files without replacing existing readiness gates.
Tests and docs cover the new installed behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: Installed repos can define area contracts in a structured local file and the kit installs a useful default/example without making unknown target areas fail adoption.
repo_contract_kit.py goal-check or equivalent emits JSON and text mapping changed files to area contracts, including aligned, extends, conflict, and unknown states.
The installed Make target exposes the report and is documented in workflow help/operator docs.
agent-start packets include a goal-check or area-goal summary for current changed files.
agent-task-ready includes goal-check status for the task worktree's actual changed files and warns/blocks according to the accepted design without weakening existing readiness blockers.
Task-packet scaffolds include valid goal_alignment fields that can be populated from the deterministic report or explicit unknowns.
acceptance_criteria.verification: CLI tests and installed task-packet schema/prompt snapshots.

acceptance_criteria.description: VERSION and CHANGELOG.md in repo-contract-kit record the new installed behavior.
Source backlog rows and summary close AGW-091 only after kit validation passes.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_agent_start tests.test_agent_task_ready tests.test_install
make backlog-check && make backlog-split-check && PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make agent-verify && git diff --check
commands.expected_result: pass in source repo after backlog closeout

evidence_to_capture: source backlog check and agent-verify output after closeout

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-091 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable for this non-worktree parent-run packet
readiness_check.expected_result: ready report passes or blocker is recorded

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finish TASK=AGW-091 TASK_RECEIPT=<path> TASK_LIFECYCLE_JSON=1 or lifecycle fallback
lifecycle_action.expected_result: metadata is closed and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is durable

final_task_status.expected_result: task is terminal or no local metadata was created and the reason is recorded

closeout_preview.expected_result: eligible, retained, or blocked cleanup state is recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether each checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This adds installed CLI/Make behavior and a managed target-repo contract file.

known_risks: Path matching can give false confidence if broad globs shadow specific area contracts.
Blocking unknown areas too early could stop useful adoption in repos without area contracts; unknowns should be visible and configurable.
Task-packet schema/prompt snapshots in the kit can drift from the source AGW-090 contract if only the CLI is updated.
Integrating with readiness must not weaken existing scope drift, receipt, docs-impact, freshness, or overlap gates.
stop_conditions: The implementation requires semantic LLM classification instead of deterministic path/config matching.
The work expands into AGW-092 compact context bundles or AGW-095 dirty-primary baselines.
Updating installed task-packet prompt/schema snapshots requires unreviewed source prompt changes rather than consuming the already-published AGW-090 state.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to implement remaining backlog items serially with one task agent per item.

overlap_warnings: This task edits the companion kit checkout plus source backlog records; keep commits separate by repo.

token_budget.guidance: Prefer a compact deterministic report over copying broad docs into every startup or task packet.

dependencies: Existing kit path classification and changed-file helpers in check_doc_impact.py, repo_contract_kit.py, agent_start.py, and agent_task_ready.py.
handoff.next_packet_hint: AGW-092 should compose this report into a compact context bundle with other deterministic startup and handoff signals.
